articles.title: Earthquake is devastating blow to Venezuela at time of uncertainty
articles.summary: The twin quakes come less than six months after Venezuela's then-leader Nicolás Maduro was seized by US forces.

articles.title: People flee as building collapses on Caracas outskirts
articles.summary: The capital and its surrounding areas have seen some of the worst damage from the 7.2 and 7.5 quakes, which hit a minute apart.

articles.title: France warns even young people's health at risk as Europe's heatwave shifts east
articles.summary: Temperatures in Germany could hit 40C in some areas while French officials say deaths linked to the heat are being seen among young people.

articles.title: UN pauses Strait of Hormuz evacuation plan after cargo ship attacked
articles.summary: The ship was reportedly struck by an "unknown projectile" near Oman but no casualties were reported.

articles.title: Women alleging rape and sexual assault in France call to abolish statute of limitations
articles.summary: More than 50 women are calling for the abolition of the statute of limitations which they say has prevented them seeking justice.

articles.title: Trump asks Congress for billions for Iran war, after tension with Republicans
articles.summary: But the budget faces an uphill battle as the president spars with some members of his own party over the issue.

articles.title: Key figure in South Africa police corruption scandal pleads guilty
articles.summary: Prosecutors say Vusimusi "Cat" Matlala could provide evidence against "high-ranking officials".

articles.title: Oil price falls back to pre-Iran war levels
articles.summary: Signs that traffic through the key Strait of Hormuz shipping route is gradually resuming has helped to push the oil price down.

articles.title: IBM hails new 'block of flats' design breakthrough for ultra tiny chips
articles.summary: IBM says it has created the world's first known chip tech below 1 nanometre - but it will be some time before it's ready for production.

articles.title: Ryanair says it will reluctantly not charge parents to sit next to children
articles.summary: The airline had typically charged adults a fee of £8 each way to sit with their young children.
